title: Cat Loose Stool After Changing Food Brand: Triage and Care Guide
meta_desc: Is your cat experiencing loose stools after a food change? Learn why this happens, why it is usually low urgency, and how a photo helps triage.
content_html:
Understanding Dietary Transitions

It is very common for cats to experience mild digestive upset when their diet is changed abruptly. A cat's gastrointestinal tract contains a delicate balance of bacteria that helps break down specific ingredients. When you introduce a new brand, the gut may need time to adapt to different protein levels, fiber content, or fat sources, often resulting in loose stools.

Is It an Emergency?

Urgency Level: Low. If your cat is otherwise acting normally—meaning they are playful, hydrated, and have a good appetite—this is typically not an emergency. Most cases of transition-related loose stool resolve on their own within a few days. However, if the stool is accompanied by frequent vomiting, extreme lethargy, or if your cat stops eating entirely, you should contact a veterinarian.

How a Photo Helps Triage

While it may feel unusual, taking a clear photo of your cat's stool can be an invaluable tool for your veterinary team. A photo allows a professional to assess the stool's consistency using the Purina Fecal Scoring System, check for the presence of mucus or blood, and look for signs of malabsorption. This visual evidence helps us determine if your cat needs a prescription diet, a slower transition, or a clinical examination.

faqs.question: How long does loose stool last after a food change?
faqs.answer: Most cats adjust to new food within 3 to 5 days. If the loose stool persists longer than a week, consult your veterinarian.

faqs.question: What is the best way to transition cat food?
faqs.answer: A gradual transition over 7 to 10 days is recommended, slowly increasing the ratio of new food to old food each day.

faqs.question: Should I stop feeding the new food if my cat has loose stools?
faqs.answer: If the stool is only mildly soft, you can try slowing down the transition. If it is watery diarrhea, it is best to go back to the old food and seek advice.
